title: /feedback command opt-out
why_it_matters: Running Claude Code's /feedback command sends a copy of the conversation history, including code, to Anthropic; the user chooses how much history to include before submitting (the current session only, by default, or additional sessions from the same project over a longer window), and transcripts shared this way are retained for 5 years. Submitted feedback is stored in Google Cloud Storage, which Anthropic's documentation states encrypts data at rest by default, and may optionally file a GitHub issue in Anthropic's public repository. The command is on by default for direct Claude API usage and off by default for every alternative model-provider integration documented in Anthropic's 'Default behaviors by API provider' table (Google Cloud's Agent Platform, Amazon Bedrock, Microsoft Foundry, and Claude Platform on AWS). Setting the environment variable DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND=1 disables the /feedback command outright. Anthropic's settings reference documents no dedicated boolean settings.json key for this control; DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND is documented only as an environment variable, and the same reference states that any environment variable may be checked into settings.json's user-scope env object — so this rule observes DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND only as embedded at that path (env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND) inside the inspected user-level settings.json. It cannot observe an ambient shell- or process-level DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND set outside this file, and it does not evaluate the separate DISABLE_TELEMETRY, DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING, CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY, or CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC controls.

outcomes.message: The inspected user-level settings.json sets env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND = "1", disabling Claude Code's /feedback command.

outcomes.when: env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective /feedback command posture cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers and the ambient process environment may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND is unset in the user-level config; the /feedback command is on by default for direct Claude API usage but off by default for every alternative model-provider integration, this rule does not determine which provider is active, and uninspected managed, project, local, or CLI-flag layers plus the ambient shell environment may also set DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND outside this file.

outcomes.when: env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1"
outcomes.message: env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1" — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1" — raw values are never displayed

sources.title: Data usage
sources.notes: States, under 'Feedback using the /feedback command': 'If you choose to send us feedback about Claude Code using the /feedback command, we may use your feedback to improve our products and services. Transcripts shared via /feedback are retained for 5 years... To opt out, set the DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND environment variable to 1.' Also: submitted feedback is 'stored in Google Cloud Storage, which encrypts stored data at rest by default. Optionally, a GitHub issue is created in the public repository.' The 'Default behaviors by API provider' table documents /feedback reports as: Claude API — default on, DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND=1 to disable; Google Cloud's Agent Platform / Amazon Bedrock / Microsoft Foundry / Claude Platform on AWS — each default off. Re-fetched and re-verified 2026-07-17; content hash unchanged from the prior retrieval.

sources.title: Claude Code settings
sources.notes: Documents the top-level env settings.json key: 'Environment variables applied to every session,' example {"FOO": "bar"}, and documents no dedicated boolean /feedback-command key. Also documents the user scope resolving to ~/.claude/settings.json (lowest precedence) — the only scope this rule inspects. Re-fetched and re-verified 2026-07-17; content hash unchanged from the prior retrieval.

limitations: Only the user-level ~/.claude/settings.json env object is inspected; managed, project, local, and CLI-flag scopes are not inspected and can supply or override the effective value, and this rule cannot observe DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND set directly in the shell or process environment outside settings.json.

Default /feedback command posture is provider-dependent (on by default for direct Claude API usage; off by default for Google Cloud's Agent Platform, Amazon Bedrock, Microsoft Foundry, and Claude Platform on AWS); this rule does not determine which provider is active and never infers the effective default from account or environment state.

This rule observes only whether the /feedback command is disabled outright; it does not determine whether the command has ever actually been invoked, nor how much session history a user chose to include when submitting feedback.

This rule observes only the /feedback command opt-out; usage-metrics telemetry (DISABLE_TELEMETRY), error reporting (DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING), session quality surveys (CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY), and the broader CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC switch are separate documented controls this rule does not evaluate.

No settings.json key named env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND appears as a worked example in either retrieved page; this rule relies on the documented, general statement that any environment variable may be checked into settings.json's env object. Any value other than the documented "1" opt-out token has no confirmed documented effect and is treated as unrecognized rather than assumed to enable or disable the command.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers are not inspected.
env.DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND set to a value other than "1" (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
